patterns.name: FastAPI Auth Pattern
keyFacts: Use OAuth2PasswordBearer for token extraction from headers
Create a get_current_user dependency that decodes JWT
Inject with Depends(get_current_user) on protected routes
Use python-jose for JWT: jose.jwt.encode/decode
Hash passwords with passlib: CryptContext(schemes=['bcrypt'])
Return 401 with WWW-Authenticate header on auth failure
patterns.snippet: async def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme), db: Session = Depends(get_db)): payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])

patterns.name: SQLAlchemy Session Pattern
keyFacts: Always use context managers for sessions: with SessionLocal() as db:
Or use generator dependency: def get_db(): db = SessionLocal(); try: yield db; finally: db.close()
Add db.commit() before returning in write operations
Use db.rollback() in except blocks
db.refresh(obj) after commit to reload auto-generated fields like id
Use .options(joinedload()) to eagerly load relationships
patterns.snippet: def get_db(): db = SessionLocal(); try: yield db; finally: db.close()

patterns.name: Pydantic v2 Pattern
keyFacts: Pydantic v2: use model_validate() not parse_obj()
Use model_dump() not .dict()
Field validators use @field_validator decorator
Model validators use @model_validator(mode='after')
Use model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) for ORM mode (not orm_mode=True)

patterns.name: Async Python Pattern
keyFacts: Use asyncio.run(main()) as entry point — not loop.run_until_complete()
Use async with for async context managers (aiohttp, databases)
Use asyncio.gather() for parallel coroutines
Don't use time.sleep() in async code — use await asyncio.sleep()
Sync blocking calls in async code: run in executor: await loop.run_in_executor(None, blocking_fn)

patterns.name: Python Error Handling Pattern
keyFacts: Catch specific exceptions, not bare except:
Use logging.exception() inside except to capture stack trace
Create custom exceptions inheriting from Exception for domain errors
Re-raise with context: raise NewException('msg') from original_exc
Use contextlib.suppress(ExceptionType) for expected suppressible errors
patterns.snippet: except ValueError as e: logger.exception('Validation failed'); raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(e)) from e
